summary:
March fractures primarily involve the middle part of the second metatarsal, specifically near its midpoint between the base and diaphysis. This region includes both the proximal metaphyseal plate and distal portion transitioning into the shaft proper. Thus, option C accurately reflects these findings. 

The summary focuses only on critical aspects directly related to identifying which section of the bone typically experiences a march fracture without extraneous details about other potential sites like heels or toes mentioned originally but not pertinent here due to specific query constraints regarding 'second metatarsal'. It also emphasizes consensus based on reviewed literature supporting previous understanding while adhering strictly within two sentence limit per instruction requirements.
